title: Joining Arrays
description:
Consider two arrays of integers A‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ[a1,‚ÄČa2,‚ÄČ...,‚ÄČan] and B‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ[b1,‚ÄČb2,‚ÄČ...,‚ÄČbm]. Let us introduce their k-join as a lexicographically smallest array R of length k, that can be divided to two non-empty subsequences, first of which is a subsequence of A, and the second one is a subsequence of B.

Formally speaking, you need to find such array R‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ[r1,‚ÄČr2,‚ÄČ...,‚ÄČrk], that there are two non-empty sequences of indices 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČi1,1‚ÄČ<‚ÄČi1,2‚ÄČ<‚ÄČ...‚ÄČ<‚ÄČi1,t‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn and 1‚ÄČ<‚ÄČj1,1‚ÄČ<‚ÄČj1,2‚ÄČ<‚ÄČ...‚ÄČ<‚ÄČj1,k-t‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČm in the original arrays, and two sequences of indices 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČi2,1‚ÄČ<‚ÄČi2,2‚ÄČ<‚ÄČ...‚ÄČ<‚ÄČi2,t‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČk and 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČj2,1‚ÄČ<‚ÄČj2,2‚ÄČ<‚ÄČ...‚ÄČ<‚ÄČj2,k-t‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČk, such that:

  • For each 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČp‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČt, 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČq‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČk‚ÄČ-‚ÄČt we have i2,p‚ÄČ‚Č†‚ÄČj2,q;
  • For each 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČp‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČt we have ai1,p‚ÄČ=‚ÄČri1,p;
  • For each 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČp‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČk‚ÄČ-‚ÄČt we have bj1,p‚ÄČ=‚ÄČrj1,p.

For example if A‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ[1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ3,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ1], B‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ[1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ3,‚ÄČ1], and k‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ9, their k-join is the array R‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ[1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ3,‚ÄČ1] (subsequence from the first array ‚ÄĒ [1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ1], subsequence from the second array ‚ÄĒ [1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ3,‚ÄČ1]).

For two given arrays A and B, and an integer k find their k-join R.
input:
The first line of input contains n ‚ÄĒ length of array A (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3000).

The second line contains n integers ai ‚ÄĒ array A (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČai‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3000).

The third line contains m ‚ÄĒ length of array B (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČm‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3000).

The fourth line contains m integers bi ‚ÄĒ array B (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČbi‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3000).

The last line contains integer k (2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČk‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn‚ÄČ+‚ÄČm).
source: Contest, Russian Code Cup, 2017, RCC 2017 Elimination Round
